mechanism_narrative: A rise in the housing market signal, as indicated by a surge in HOUST, triggers an increase in demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in housing market downturns are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signal, as indicated by a rise in UNRATE, triggers an increase in labor costs and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recessionary periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p  0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signal, as indicated by a drop in INDPRO, triggers an increase in supply chain disruptions and reduced economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recessionary periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p  0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the retail sales signal, as indicated by a surge in PAYEMS, triggers an increase in demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signal, as indicated by a drop in FEDFUNDS, triggers an increase in discount rates applied to equity cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the GDP signal, as indicated by a surge in GDPC1, triggers an increase in aggregate demand and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the labor force participation signal, as indicated by a surge in INDPRO, triggers an increase in labor costs and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recessionary periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p  0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the retail sales signal, as indicated by a drop in RSAFS, triggers an increase in supply chain disruptions and reduced economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recessionary periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
